sn9.5:1.1: On one occasion Ven. Ānanda was staying among the Kosalans in a forest grove.
sn9.5:1.2: Now at that time, he was spending too much time devoted to instructing laypeople.
sn9.5:1.3: Then the devatā inhabiting the forest grove, feeling sympathy for Ven. Ānanda, desiring his benefit, desiring to bring him to his senses, approached him and recited a verse:
sn9.5:2.1: “Going to the bower at the root of a tree,
sn9.5:2.3: do jhāna, Gotama, & don’t be heedless.
sn9.5:2.4: What use is this chitter-chatter to you?”
sn9.5:3.1: Then Ven. Ānanda, chastened by the devatā, came to his senses.
